verdict_reasoning: HSPD1/HSP60 is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin essential for folding imported proteins. However, an ecto-HSP60 pool is well documented on the surface of stressed and tumor cells, where it acts as a danger signal engaging innate immune receptors (TLR2/4) and is recognized by anti-tumor antibodies. Cancer-cell and stress-induced surface display qualifies as cell_state_induced contextual accessibility. Not a TM/GPI protein at baseline; recruitment to outer leaflet is condition-dependent.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Magnitude and reproducibility of surface HSP60 pool across tumor types; some reports attribute signal to EV-associated rather than PM-anchored protein.
